_provenance.purpose: GOAP plan A1 — a measured snapshot of AQE's DE-FACTO MCP governance posture, translated into the .harness/mcp-policy.json shape that MetaHarness `harness mcp-scan` reads, so the scanner enumerates AQE's real surface instead of reporting 'MCP not enabled' (AQE registers servers in .mcp.json, which mcp-scan does not read).
_provenance.notATarget: This file MIRRORS current reality to surface true findings. It is NOT a hand-tuned default-deny target. The remediation step (authoring an actual default-deny allowlist) is a separate deliverable.
sources: src/mcp/tools/registry.ts (QE_TOOLS: 30 core tool instances + ...MINCUT_TOOL_NAMES + ...COHERENCE_TOOL_NAMES; ~90 exposed via src/mcp/qe-tool-bridge.ts)
src/mcp/tool-scoping.ts (per-role allowlists incl. allowAll admin tiers)

_defaultDeny_evidence: No default-deny posture exists. tool-scoping.ts grants two roles (fleet-admin, unrestricted) allowAll:true; isToolAllowed returns true wholesale for them. .claude/settings.json is allow-list based (ungranted tools prompt, are not denied). Matches `harness score` finding 'default-deny OFF'.
_allowShell_evidence: QE_TOOLS includes test_execute_parallel and chaos_test, which spawn test/runtime processes. No policy gates this.
_allowNetwork_evidence: QE_TOOLS includes qe/security/url-validate, embeddings (ONNX/model fetch), and model_route, which reach the network. No host-scoping.
_allowFileWrite_evidence: QE_TOOLS includes test_generate_enhanced (writes test files) and report/coverage tools. No write-scoping in any MCP policy.
_requireApprovalForDangerous_evidence: No approval gate exists on the MCP execution path; dangerous tools (chaos_test, test_execute) run without explicit consent.
_auditLog_evidence: src/audit/witness-chain.ts records INTERNAL events only (per GOAP plan A7); MCP tool-call auditing is not wired. Conservatively false until verified.
_toolTimeoutMs_evidence: No per-tool timeout configured in any MCP policy.

roleAllowlists._note: Verbatim from src/mcp/tool-scoping.ts DEFAULT_SCOPES. The two allowAll tiers are the predicted HIGH (over-broad grant).
